title: Frontier AI Release Governance Public-Source Docket
case: Anthropic Fable/Mythos and OpenAI GPT-5.6 public-source release-governance pattern
claim_boundary: No model call is performed by the website console.
No wallet, transaction, settlement, external API call, or production activation is performed.
No legal, export-control, safety, security, or investment advice is provided.
No claim is made that GoalOS resolves any specific company-government dispute.
Human authority and independent review remain required for real decisions.
bounded_claim: The public-source record supports the need for a structured Evidence Docket process for high-consequence frontier AI release decisions.
unsupported_claims: GoalOS certifies any model as safe.
GoalOS replaces government, legal, or export-control review.
GoalOS has inside knowledge of confidential release decisions.

mission_contract.objective: Convert a public frontier-release governance controversy into a structured, source-cited, validator-reviewable docket.

required_real_evidence_for_stronger_clai: legal and policy review by qualified counsel

sources.title: Fable and Mythos access update
sources.used_for: Anthropic's public statement on access suspension, foreign-national scope, and its position on transparent, fair, technically grounded government action.

sources.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol
sources.used_for: OpenAI's public framing of a limited trusted-partner preview and government engagement around a frontier model release.

sources.title: OpenAI defers public rollout of GPT-5.6 as US seeks early access to frontier AI models
sources.used_for: Public reporting on the government-requested staged release pattern and broader national-security context.

sources.title: Anthropic's Mythos mess is only getting worse
sources.used_for: Public reporting on the unresolved standoff and the absence of a clear operational rulebook.

sources.title: OpenAI limits its latest ChatGPT product to approved customers during cybersecurity review
sources.used_for: Public reporting on limited preview access, cybersecurity review, and stakeholder concerns.

sources.title: AI Risk Management Framework
sources.used_for: General risk-management context for govern, map, measure, and manage functions.

sources.title: ISO/IEC 42001 Artificial intelligence management system
sources.used_for: AI management-system context for institutional governance practices.

sources.title: Components of frontier AI safety frameworks
sources.used_for: Context on frontier safety frameworks, risk thresholds, evaluations, and mitigation strategies.
